title: Swapping two cups — the Python way
problem: Cup a holds juice and cup b holds milk. Swap them, so cup a ends with milk and cup b ends with juice.
source:
a = "juice"
b = "milk"
a, b = b, a

trace.note: Cup a (the left cup) holds juice.
Cup b (the right cup) holds milk.

Python picks up both cups at once, swaps them in the air, and sets them back down. One line, no spare cup: cup a now holds milk, cup b holds juice. (Under the hood, the right side b, a is packed into a pair, then unpacked into a and b.)
